title: mariscus angustifolius
intro: Mariscus angustifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved marsh marigold, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant is known to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Mariscus angustifolius can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other minor health issues.
